titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in L32
GA L32
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used were version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Word break removed at 8.4 by David Parker.
Verse break corrected at S1W7D2 and S5W1D5i 14.29-30 by David Parker.
Word break corrected at S1W4D6 8.28 by Rachel Kevern.
Beginning of 21.15-2 corrected to 21.14 by Rachel Kevern.
Word break at 18.39 corrected by Rachel Kevern.

body.note: This base text is divided into three sections:1. The Synaxarion. Readings are identified by 'S' for 'Synaxarion', 'W' for 'Week' and 'D' for 'Day' 2. Resurrection Readings are identified by 'R' for 'Resurrection3. The Menologion. Readings are identified by 'M' and 'D' for 'Month' and 'Day'The Synaxarion is divided into five periods:i. Pascha to Pentecost: S1 W1 D1 – S1 W8 D1ii. Pentecost to the Elevation of the Cross: S2 W1 D2 – S2 W18 D2iii. Elevation of the Cross to Lent: S3 W1 D2 – S3 W20 D1iv. Lent to Holy Week: S4 D1 D2 – S4 W7 D1cv. Holy Week: S5 W1 D2a – S5 W1 D7bStatus:G-Α number: L32 Νame of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: P2Βase text: TRnsΤranscribed by: Εirini PanouΤranscription begun: 20 December 2012Τranscription finished: 24 December 2012Transcription P227-end by acm.Lectionary title top margin tag added throughout by acm. Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate):Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes Diples (> or >> in the margin next to certain text, indicating that text is a quotation from the Old Testament) - No Are there any special symbols in the margin next to any of the following verses: 5.4 No 7.53 - 8.11 No Other remarks: Many spelling mistakes. Iota adscript usedTwo unidentified lections:U1 P423 Jn 8,3-11 M2 D8?U2 P493 Jn 10,1-9 M9 D12?
1. συναξαριoν i: πασχα to πεντηκoστη - ευαγγελια κατα ιωαννην - S1 W1 D1 - S1 W8 D1 Easter to Pentecost

div.note: John 20,19-31 Second Sunday of Ρascha: Τhomas Sunday
The beginning of this lection (5.24ff. is lacunose.
The beginning of this lection (15.17) is lacunose.
John 5,1-15 Third Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Ρaralytic
John 4,5-42 Fifth Sunday of Ρascha: Sunday of the Samaritan Woman

ab.note: An alternative reading for the Feast of Dedication.

div.note: John 7,37-52; 8,12 Ρentecost (λειτoυργια) The pericope adulterae is omitted in all known copies on this Feast Day

ab.note: lectionary incipit is in the bottom margin: αρχη τω καιρω εκεινω επαρας ο ις̅ τους οφθαλμους αυτου εις τους μαθητας αυτου ειπεν

div.note: The heading instructs the user to read this pericope on the Sunday before the 14th September, the Feast of the Elevation of the Cross.
Our lectionary index has no Gospel reading on this day.
Our lectionary index has no Gospel reading on this day.
